worlds.name: Interview bank I
worlds.subtitle: Filters & counts
worlds.description: Filters & counts — 30 original SQL drills on the Summit commerce schema (Easy–Medium patterns common in interviews). Not affiliated with any third-party problem site.

levels.title: Paid orders for account 1
levels.story: Ops needs a quick count of paid checkouts for one customer id.
levels.task: Single column n — paid orders for this customer only.
levels.expectedQuery: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM orders WHERE status = 'paid' AND customer_id = 1;
levels.solveGuide: Filter orders by status and customer_id, then COUNT(*).

levels.title: Units moved for SKU id 1
levels.story: Inventory wants total quantity sold across all orders for one product.
levels.task: One row: units = SUM(qty) for lines with this product_id.
levels.expectedQuery: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(qty), 0) AS units FROM order_lines WHERE product_id = 1;
levels.solveGuide: SUM(qty) with WHERE on product_id; COALESCE handles no rows.

levels.title: Distinct orders touching product 1
levels.story: How many different orders include at least one line for this product?
levels.task: Column order_cnt — COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) for lines with this product.
levels.expectedQuery: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS order_cnt FROM order_lines WHERE product_id = 1;
levels.solveGuide: COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) after filtering lines.

levels.title: Account name lookup
levels.story: Finance pasted id 1 from a ticket — return the legal name.
levels.task: Single column name for this customer id.
levels.expectedQuery: SELECT name FROM customers WHERE id = 1;
levels.solveGuide: Simple SELECT … FROM customers WHERE id = …

levels.title: Net line revenue for order 101
levels.story: Discount-aware revenue for one order (qty × list × (1 − discount%)).
levels.task: One row line_rev — SUM discounted line dollars for this order_id.
levels.expectedQuery:
SELECT ROUND(SUM(ol.qty * p.list_price * (1 - ol.discount_pct / 100.0)), 2) AS line_rev
FROM order_lines ol
JOIN products p ON ol.product_id = p.id
WHERE ol.order_id = 101;
levels.solveGuide: Join lines to products; SUM qty * list_price * (1 - discount_pct/100).

levels.title: Headcount — starter tier
levels.story: GTM segmentation: how many accounts are on this contract tier?
levels.task: Single column n — customers where tier = 'starter'.
levels.expectedQuery: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM customers WHERE tier = 'starter';
levels.solveGuide: COUNT(*) FROM customers with tier filter.

levels.title: Region code for customer
levels.story: Map a customer id to their region code for routing.
levels.task: Single column code — region code for this customer.
levels.expectedQuery:
SELECT r.code
FROM customers c
JOIN regions r ON r.id = c.region_id
WHERE c.id = 1;
levels.solveGuide: customers JOIN regions ON region_id = regions.id

levels.title: Priciest HW SKU
levels.story: Within category 'hw', which sku has the highest list price?
levels.task: One row: sku — top list_price in this category.
levels.expectedQuery: SELECT sku FROM products WHERE category = 'hw' ORDER BY list_price DESC LIMIT 1;
levels.solveGuide: WHERE category, ORDER BY list_price DESC, LIMIT 1.

levels.title: Exactly 1 paid order(s)
levels.story: Lifecycle tag: accounts with a precise paid-order frequency.
levels.task: id, name for customers with exactly 1 paid orders; ORDER BY id.
levels.expectedQuery:
SELECT c.id, c.name
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id
WHERE o.status = 'paid'
GROUP BY c.id, c.name
HAVING COUNT(*) = 1
ORDER BY c.id;
levels.solveGuide: Join customers to orders, filter paid, GROUP BY customer, HAVING COUNT = n.

levels.title: Busy baskets (2+ lines)
levels.story: Fulfillment wants orders with at least this many line rows.
levels.task: order_id and line_cnt where line_cnt >= 2; ORDER BY order_id.
levels.expectedQuery:
SELECT order_id, COUNT(*) AS line_cnt
FROM order_lines
GROUP BY order_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
ORDER BY order_id;
levels.solveGuide: GROUP BY order_id, HAVING COUNT(*) >= k.

levels.title: No paid history
levels.story: Risk list: customers who have never completed a paid order.
levels.task: id, name — no paid order ever; ORDER BY id.
levels.expectedQuery:
SELECT c.id, c.name
FROM customers c
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 FROM orders o
  WHERE o.customer_id = c.id AND o.status = 'paid'
)
ORDER BY c.id;
levels.solveGuide: NOT EXISTS correlated subquery on orders with the right status filter.

levels.title: Refunded units — product 1
levels.story: Units attached to refunded orders, filtered to one product line.
levels.task: One row refunded_units — SUM(qty) for refunded orders and product_id 1.
levels.expectedQuery:
SELECT SUM(ol.qty) AS refunded_units
FROM order_lines ol
JOIN orders o ON o.id = ol.order_id
WHERE o.status = 'refunded' AND ol.product_id = 1;
levels.solveGuide: Join lines to orders; filter refunded and product.

levels.title: First paid order — region 1
levels.story: Within one region, earliest paid order id per customer who has paid.
levels.task: id, first_order_id for customers in region_id 1 with paid orders; ORDER BY id.
levels.expectedQuery:
SELECT c.id, MIN(o.id) AS first_order_id
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id AND o.status = 'paid'
WHERE c.region_id = 1
GROUP BY c.id
ORDER BY c.id;
levels.solveGuide: Join customers to paid orders, filter region, GROUP BY customer, MIN(order id).

levels.title: Above average hw price
levels.story: Pricing review: SKUs in this category above category mean list price.
levels.task: sku where category 'hw' and list_price > subquery AVG for same category; ORDER BY sku.
levels.expectedQuery:
SELECT p.sku
FROM products p
WHERE p.category = 'hw'
  AND p.list_price > (SELECT AVG(list_price) FROM products WHERE category = 'hw')
ORDER BY p.sku;
levels.solveGuide: Scalar subquery AVG(list_price) … WHERE category matches.

levels.title: Warehouse footprint — region 1
levels.story: How many warehouse rows are attached to this region?
levels.task: name, warehouse_cnt for region id 1 (LEFT JOIN warehouses).
levels.expectedQuery:
SELECT r.name, COUNT(w.id) AS warehouse_cnt
FROM regions r
LEFT JOIN warehouses w ON w.region_id = r.id
WHERE r.id = 1
GROUP BY r.id, r.name;
levels.solveGuide: regions LEFT JOIN warehouses ON region_id; COUNT(w.id).

levels.title: On-hand above 0
levels.story: Products whose snapshot totals exceed a stock threshold.
levels.task: product_id, total_on_hand — SUM(on_hand) per product HAVING sum > 0; ORDER BY product_id.
levels.expectedQuery:
SELECT i.product_id, SUM(i.on_hand) AS total_on_hand
FROM inventory_snapshot i
GROUP BY i.product_id
HAVING SUM(i.on_hand) > 0
ORDER BY i.product_id;
levels.solveGuide: GROUP BY product_id, HAVING SUM(on_hand) > threshold.

levels.title: Discount burn rank #1
levels.story: Nth ranked paid order by total discount dollars given away on lines.
levels.task: order_id, discount_dollars — rank paid orders by discount sum DESC, order_id ASC; OFFSET 0.
levels.expectedQuery:
SELECT ol.order_id,
  SUM(ol.qty * p.list_price * (ol.discount_pct / 100.0)) AS discount_dollars
FROM order_lines ol
JOIN products p ON ol.product_id = p.id
JOIN orders o ON ol.order_id = o.id
WHERE o.status = 'paid'
GROUP BY ol.order_id
ORDER BY discount_dollars DESC, ol.order_id ASC
LIMIT 1 OFFSET 0;
levels.solveGuide: Per-order discount SUM, ORDER BY DESC, LIMIT 1 OFFSET n.

levels.title: Nth best seller by units
levels.story: Ranking drill: pick the product at this offset by total quantity (0 = top).
levels.task: Single column product_id — rank all products by SUM(qty) desc, product_id asc; OFFSET 0.
levels.expectedQuery:
SELECT product_id FROM (
  SELECT product_id, SUM(qty) AS q
  FROM order_lines
  GROUP BY product_id
  ORDER BY q DESC, product_id ASC
  LIMIT 1 OFFSET 0
);
constraints: subquery with ORDER BY q DESC, product_id ASC
levels.solveGuide: Inner query GROUP BY product_id, ORDER BY sum desc, LIMIT 1 OFFSET n.

levels.title: Tied for most paid — region 1
levels.story: Within this region, every customer tied for the highest paid-order count.
levels.task: customer_id only — ties for max paid count among customers in region 1; ORDER BY customer_id.
levels.expectedQuery:
WITH rc AS (
  SELECT c.id AS customer_id, COUNT(*) AS n
  FROM customers c
  JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id AND o.status = 'paid'
  WHERE c.region_id = 1
  GROUP BY c.id
)
SELECT customer_id FROM rc
WHERE n = (SELECT MAX(n) FROM rc)
ORDER BY customer_id;
levels.solveGuide: Scope CTE to region; MAX(n) within that subset.

levels.title: HW + SVC — region 1
levels.story: In one region, customers with paid hardware and paid service lines.
levels.task: DISTINCT id, name — region 1, paid hw and paid svc; ORDER BY id.
levels.expectedQuery:
SELECT DISTINCT c.id, c.name
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id AND o.status = 'paid'
JOIN order_lines ol ON ol.order_id = o.id
JOIN products p ON p.id = ol.product_id
WHERE c.region_id = 1
  AND p.category = 'hw'
  AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM orders o2
    JOIN order_lines ol2 ON ol2.order_id = o2.id
    JOIN products p2 ON p2.id = ol2.product_id
    WHERE o2.customer_id = c.id AND o2.status = 'paid' AND p2.category = 'svc'
  )
ORDER BY c.id;
levels.solveGuide: Filter region on outer customer; EXISTS for svc on same customer.

levels.title: Discount burn rank #2
levels.story: Nth ranked paid order by total discount dollars given away on lines.
levels.task: order_id, discount_dollars — rank paid orders by discount sum DESC, order_id ASC; OFFSET 1.

JOIN orders o ON ol.order_id = o.id

ORDER BY discount_dollars DESC, ol.order_id ASC
LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1;
